title: Anacamptis × parvifolia
taxonomy: Anacamptis × parvifolia is a hybrid orchid species resulting from a cross between Anacamptis morio and Anacamptis papillonacea. This rare orchid is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is considered an endangered species. It belongs to the genus Anacamptis within the family Orchidaceae. Anacamptis × parvifolia is a protected species due to its limited distribution and habitat. Its classification as a hybrid orchid makes it unique among the species in the Anacamptis genus.
uses: Anacamptis × parvifolia, also known as the hybrid green helleborine, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. Its roots and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Anacamptis × parvifolia as a medicinal plant. More studies are required to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.

intro: Anacamptis × parvifolia, also known as the Pyramidal Orchid hybrid, is a rare and endangered orchid species that is a hybrid of the Pyramidal Orchid (Anacamptis pyramidalis) and the Green-winged Orchid (Anacamptis morio). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Anacamptis × parvifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Anacamptis × parvifolia is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in the production of cough syrups and other respiratory remedies.
